decisive_pair_note: Layer-1 shape and both branch cards (curl_129_h1, curl_165_h1) name the decisive pair as fast>minimizer (aflfast_rarity). The dispatch prompt framed it as 'fast vs naive'; I follow the signatures/cards (fast RESOLVES, minimizer BLOCKS). minimizer = PowerSchedule None (calibration-only energy); fast = AFLFast FAST n_fuzz rare-edge power schedule. Substitute naive for minimizer only if the campaign lacks a minimizer arm and naive is the confirmed flat-energy baseline.

hypotheses.label: fast over-invests havoc energy in the rare-edge seed, so its corpus shows more exploration past the rare edge than minimizer's

measurement.registry_tool_note: No existing registry tool measures scheduling energy allocation. operand_enrichment is for fixed-offset VALUE gates (this is a presence/non-null gate, no literal); joint_necessity is for vpc assembly-depth. NEW descriptor proposed; orchestrator builds it.

params.rare_edge_seed: the lowest-n_fuzz seed in fast's corpus that hits the gate's caller edge (Curl_http_host @ http.c:2157 for curl_129; Curl_http_cookies @ http.c:2792 for curl_165) but NOT the target branch
params.descendant_share: fraction of corpus seeds that are lineage-descendants of the rare-edge seed (or, if lineage parentage is unavailable, fraction whose covered-edge set is a strict superset of the rare-edge seed's) — computed identically for fast and minimizer corpora, expressed as fast / minimizer ratio
params.depth_reach: max coverage-edge depth past the rare edge reached by any descendant, fast minus minimizer

alternative_descriptor.use_when: seed_lineage parentage is missing for the fast/minimizer corpora; fall back to the per-trial coverage-timeseries growth rate of edges in the rare-edge region (functions reachable only past the gate's caller) as fast-vs-minimizer slope ratio

hypotheses.prediction: G1: If the win is the rare-edge POWER SCHEDULE, fast must spend disproportionate budget mutating the rare-edge seed, so fast's corpus shows MORE descendants of / deeper reach past the rare edge than minimizer's (rare_seed_descendant_share fast/minimizer >= 1.5 AND rare_seed_depth_reach > 0). A rival 'lucky seed / no scheduling effect' mechanism predicts fast and minimizer explore the rare edge equally (ratio ~ 1, depth diff ~ 0) — the win would then be variance, not the schedule. The measurement is INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): it scores corpus exploration AROUND the rare edge, not whether fast resolved the target branch.
hypotheses.decidable_rationale: G3 HONESTY: aflfast's effect is a SCHEDULING dynamic — WHICH seed gets energy WHEN — not a corpus-content fingerprint. fast and minimizer share a byte-identical havoc mutator vocabulary (per data_realities, mutation_op cannot distinguish them), so the only trace of the energy boost is corpus SCALE/SHAPE, which is confounded by run-to-run variance, total exec budget, and seed-admission luck. With n=2 branches on ONE target (curl) and no per-seed energy ledger in seed_lineage, 'more descendants past the rare edge' cannot be cleanly attributed to the power schedule vs. ordinary stochastic drift. Additionally fast/minimizer may have synthetic-harness EXECUTION issues (see notes), and mutation_op is untagged for scheduling, so no per-branch event-level confirmation exists. Mark decidable:false and let fallback carry these branches to inconclusive — an honest 'no per-branch discriminating test' beats a corpus-scale ratio the data cannot support.

data_realities_respected: Not a VALUE gate (presence_or_nonnull, operand_literal null) -> operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) inapplicable; not proposed.
Not a vpc joint-assembly gate -> joint_necessity inapplicable; not proposed.
mutation_op is byte-identical / untagged across fuzzers -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence 'scheduling event' descriptor proposed (it would be infeasible like the forbidden 'I2S event').
Both branches are curl, which HAS an on-disk corpus -> a corpus/lineage descriptor is at least populatable, but n=2 single-target is too thin to power the rule.
notes: Target: curl (both branches: Curl_http_host @ lib/http.c:2157, Curl_http_cookies @ lib/http.c:2792 — presence/non-null gates on a 'Host:'-bearing input reachable purely by extra havoc budget on a rare seed). WHY HARD: the mechanism is pure SCHEDULING (AFLFast FAST n_fuzz rare-edge power schedule gives rarely-hit buckets up to ~4x energy vs minimizer's PowerSchedule None calibration-only energy). Scheduling allocates energy across seeds over time; it leaves no per-branch content fingerprint — fast and minimizer save the same kinds of bytes, they just budget them differently. The only observable proxy is corpus-scale exploration past the rare edge (descendant share / depth reach), which is confounded by variance, exec budget, and admission luck, and cannot be tied to the schedule per-branch with n=2 on one target. CAVEAT: fast/minimizer are known to have execution issues on synthetic harnesses (project memory: 'fast/minimizer execute 0 on synthetic harnesses'), and mutation_op is untagged for scheduling — so neither a synthetic-template re-derivation nor a lineage event-count can confirm this. COLLAPSE: the two prior cluster members (curl_129, curl_165) describe ONE identical mechanism (rare-edge power-schedule havoc amplification on a Host-header/cookie path) — kept as a single hypothesis; nothing to discriminate between them. DECISION: decidable:false -> these branches route to inconclusive honestly (G3). If a future campaign ships a per-seed ENERGY LEDGER (exec count attributed per seed) the seed_lineage:energy_share descriptor becomes decidable and the rule can be applied; until then it is a designed-but-undecidable test.
